description: Deterministic weighted ladder-Laplacian block-encoding target. The 32 ladder qubits encode a level-16 two-rail sparse matrix; the final 10 qubits are block-encoding workspace.
name: Block-Encoding Matrix Ladder LV16

reference.width_policy: The verifier must validate candidates against an official reference for the same declared width. The stored sha256 is the full 42-qubit target reference; lower-width references must be separately registered and are never synthesized by truncating the 42-qubit target.

42.width_policy: The verifier must validate candidates against an official reference for the same declared width. The stored sha256 is the full 42-qubit target reference; lower-width references must be separately registered and are never synthesized by truncating the 42-qubit target.

references.policy: A declared width is valid only when an official same-width reference exists. Do not project a wider target by skipping terms outside the declared width.

scoring.description: Lower is better. Reference-style hardware volume: declared qubits times sqrt(weighted gate volume times weighted depth). For v1 gates, weighted gate volume is h+x+y+z plus 64*rz plus cx/cnot plus 6 per extra two-qubit distance.

trusted_shots.derivation: For declared width n, shot index s, and qubit q with 0 <= q < n, choose state_alphabet[sha256(domain_separator|target_id|n|s|q)[0:8] mod len(state_alphabet)].
